Core Concepts

Introduction: What DAM is and how it differs from plain version control.

Philosophy: The water-infrastructure naming model (Reservoir, Stream, Seal, Flow).

Core Vocabulary: DAM's own terms and definitions, standalone — not mapped against any other VCS.

.purities & .impurities: Allowlist/blocklist rule files that control which files get collected.

Project Profiles (dam.toml): Per-project config defining providers, setup commands, and named export profiles — one file powering environment checks and setup instructions for project exports.

GitHub Integration: How dam sync authenticates natively with GitHub branches using the secure credential system.

Getting Started

Quick Start: Source a reservoir, define purities, collect, seal, and safely sync to GitHub.

Common Workflows: Recipes for starting a project, saving changes, branching safely, rolling back, syncing to GitHub, and sharing a project.

FAQ: Answers on seal vs. project export, sync vs. export, credential storage, legacy migration, collection clearing, destroy safety, rule conflicts, and encryption.

Command Reference

dam source: Initialize or upgrade a reservoir (and trigger credential migration).

dam collect: Scan files into the collection area per purities/impurities rules.

dam creds: Securely manage aliases for GitHub tokens, SSH keys, or generic secrets using OS Keychain or encrypted local vaults.

dam inspect: View reservoir state, rule maps, or .dam archive metadata.

dam drain: Clear the collection area.

dam seal: Create an immutable snapshot from the collection area.

dam timeline: Print the chronological history of seals.

dam stream: Create or list streams.

dam flow: Switch the active stream.

dam apply: Restore the workspace to a given seal, with --preview.

dam sync: Push or pull seal history to/from GitHub. Automatically retrieves auth parameters from the centralized credential store.

dam export: Package a seal or an entire project for sharing.

Export a Seal: Share one historical snapshot as .seal or .zip.

Export a Project: Package the whole current project, including setup commands and provider metadata, as a portable .dam archive.

dam import: Import a .seal, .zip, or .dam archive back into a reservoir.

dam settings: View or change reservoir configuration (Note: github_token_path is deprecated/removed in v0.5.0 in favor of dam creds).

dam destroy: Permanently remove or safely archive a reservoir.

Release Notes

What's New: v0.5.0 introduces Centralized Secure Credential Management (dam creds) integrated with OS Keychain & AES-256-GCM encrypted local vaults. Includes automatic migration pipelines (dam source --upgrade) for legacy credentials and deprecates plain-text configuration variables.

Downloads

Installation: Linux (tar.gz via curl) and Windows (.zip via PowerShell or Command Prompt/curl) standalone binaries.

Optional

docs/index.html full reference: Single-page documentation containing all sections above with full command syntax, options, and examples.

DAM

DAM is a command-line Project Continuity Platform (PCP) — currently at v0.5.0, in Active Development (Alpha) — that uses its own independent vocabulary rather than Git's (e.g. "Reservoir", "Seal", "Stream", "Flow"; DAM is not a Git wrapper). It tracks, snapshots, and restores project state over time, packages an entire project — not just one snapshot — as a portable archive for handoff, and syncs a reservoir's history natively to and from GitHub branches.

DAM is built for: saving checkpoints of in-progress work, branching off to try risky changes without losing working state, backing up and sharing history via stream-aware GitHub sync, merging divergent timelines safely, and packaging a clean, runnable copy of a project to send to another person or machine.

Core Concepts

Introduction: What DAM is and how it differs from plain version control.

Philosophy: The water-infrastructure naming model (Reservoir, Stream, Seal, Flow).

Core Vocabulary: DAM's own terms and definitions, standalone — not mapped against any other VCS.

.purities & .impurities: Allowlist/blocklist rule files that control which files get collected.

Project Profiles (dam.toml): Per-project config defining providers, setup commands, and named export profiles — one file powering environment checks and setup instructions for project exports.

GitHub Integration: How dam sync maps local streams to remote branches, authenticates securely via credential manager aliases, and manages conflict resolutions.

Getting Started

Quick Start: Source a reservoir, define purities, collect, seal, and optionally sync to GitHub.

Common Workflows: Recipes for starting a project, saving changes, branching safely, merging timelines, rolling back, syncing to GitHub, and sharing a project.

FAQ: Answers on seal vs. project export, sync vs. export, 3-way merging, credential vaults, legacy migration, collection clearing, destroy safety, and encryption.

Command Reference

dam source: Initialize or upgrade a reservoir (auto-migrating legacy tokens).

dam collect: Scan files into the collection area per purities/impurities rules.

dam creds: Securely store, list, and delete credentials (GitHub tokens, SSH Keys, generic secrets) via OS Keychain or local encrypted vault.

dam inspect: View reservoir state, rule maps, or .dam archive metadata.

dam drain: Clear the collection area.

dam seal: Create an immutable snapshot from the collection area.

dam timeline: Print chronological history of seals or render the ASCII DAG visualizer (--graph).

dam stream: Manage streams with rich continuity metadata, goals, notes, and subcommands (create, inspect, goal, notes, delete).

dam flowinto: Switch the active stream context, with auto-snapshotting for unsealed staged changes.

dam merge: [Experimental] Perform a smart 3-way merge between parallel development streams and apply the results into a multi-parent seal.

dam apply: Restore the workspace to a given seal, with --preview.

dam sync: Stream-aware push/pull sync to a cloud platform. Maps streams 1:1 with branch heads, protects the main stream, and resolves interactive ahead/behind diffs using stored credential aliases.

dam export: Package a seal or an entire project for sharing.

Export a Seal: Share one historical snapshot as .seal or .zip.

Export a Project: Package the whole current project, including setup commands and provider metadata, as a portable .dam archive.

dam import: Import a .seal, .zip, or .dam archive back into a reservoir.

dam settings: View or change reservoir configuration, including rule-conflict resolution and local parameters. (Note: github_token_path has been deprecated in favor of the credentials suite).

dam destroy: Permanently remove or safely archive a reservoir.

Release Notes

What's New: v0.5.0 introduces secure credential management (dam creds) via OS Keychain and AES-256-GCM local vaults (PBKDF2-SHA256, 100,000 iterations). Supports fine-grained tokens, classic tokens, SSH keys, and generic secrets. Delivers a safe migration pipeline via dam source --upgrade to clean legacy plaintext credentials.
